What This Work Is: Non-discretionary, recurring work required to operate, secure, and support existing services. Failure creates immediate operational, security, or compliance risk.
How to Decide (Tag Here If...): Required to meet SLAs, legal, security, or risk obligations. Keeps existing services running safely and reliably. Does not introduce new business capability. Routine operational activity. Keeps model fit for use.
Representative Examples: Emergency break-fix; Prod outages; Audit evidence gathering; Monitoring, backups, DR exercises; Control execution; Model recalibration (non-regulatory).

What This Work Is: Run work driven by the business to keep existing capabilities accurate, efficient, and effective without introducing new functionality.
How to Decide (Tag Here If...): Maintains or optimizes existing business rules or processes. Driven by accuracy, usability, or efficiency. No new products or material outcome changes.
Representative Examples: Configuration updates; Process support changes; Data quality fixes; Model performance monitoring.

What This Work Is: Run work focused on technical health, stability, and efficiency of existing systems to reduce future KTLO.
How to Decide (Tag Here If...): Improves reliability, performance, or operability. Not regulation-driven. No new business functionality.
Representative Examples: Tech debt reduction; Optimization; Tool consolidation; Runbook improvements; Platform upgrades.

What This Work Is: Non-discretionary run work required to maintain compliant systems or architecture, typically time-bound.
How to Decide (Tag Here If...): Explicitly required by regulation, audit, or risk authority. Operational in nature. No architectural redesign.
Representative Examples: Regulatory reporting; Audit remediation (no redesign); HFM compliance.

What This Work Is: Non-discretionary change that materially alters systems, processes, data, or architecture due to mandates.
How to Decide (Tag Here If...): Required by regulator or audit. Requires redesign or transformation. Cannot be handled through KTLO.
Representative Examples: FHFA credit score changes; SWIFT/UDD changes; Zero-trust security architecture.

What This Work Is: Discretionary change delivering strategic outcomes aligned to enterprise strategy.
How to Decide (Tag Here If...): Aligned to enterprise strategy. Multi-team coordination. Leadership-sponsored with measurable outcomes.
Representative Examples: Loan Quality Center Transformation; AI Infrastructure Enablement; Loan Management future state.

What This Work Is: Discretionary change critical to divisional strategy but not enterprise-wide.
How to Decide (Tag Here If...): Prioritized by division leadership. Divisional-level outcomes.
Representative Examples: CDO Data & Services; Treasury Market Risk; TPES Digital Experience Transformation.

What This Work Is: Discretionary innovation work valuable but not urgent or tied to top priorities.
How to Decide (Tag Here If...): Can be deferred without material risk. Typically exploratory or small-scale.
Representative Examples: Automation pilots; Training; Reporting enhancements; Incremental improvements; AI use-case exploration.
